title: Kashmir School Holidays Extended to July 22 Amid Heatwave - Times Now
full_text:
The Jammu and Kashmir government has extended the summer vacations in Kashmir schools till July 22. Students will now return to their classes at the usual time from July 23, 2026. In case of any queries, students and their parents can directly contact their school administration.
"In view of the prevailing weather conditions, it has been decided to extend the summer vacation for all Government and recognised private schools in the Kashmir Division and the winter zones of Jammu Division up to 22nd July (Wednesday)," Education Minister Sakina Ittoo posted on her X handle.
The government had announced the summer vacations on July 6, 2026, in view of intense heatwave conditions. The schools were scheduled to reopen on July 18, but the persistent heatwaves forced the authorities to announce closures.
Himachal Pradesh Schools Closed Till July 21 Amid IMD Red Alert
Meanwhile, all government and private educational institutions in Himachal Pradesh's Kangra, Mandi and Sirmaur districts will remain closed on July 20 and 21 in view of a red alert issued by the weather department, according to the PTI reports.
The Met Office has issued a red warning for heavy rains in Chamba, Kangra, Mandi and Sirmaur districts on Monday and for Chamba, Kangra, Kullu, Mandi and Sirmaur districts on Tuesday.
The order applies to all schools, colleges, vocational and technical training institutes, anganwadi centres, creches, the Central University of Himachal Pradesh, and the Sanskrit University at Balyahar in Kangra district.
Furthermore, Bairwa directed the deferral of examinations scheduled for July 20 and 21, 2026. "While teaching and non-teaching staff will remain off duty, their services may be requisitioned by the district administration for emergency or disaster management duties if required, he said," according to the news agency.
For the latest updates on school closures across India, keep visiting https://www.timesnownews.com/education.
